title: TrueNAS ZFS ARC hit ratio and memory pressure trending
dataSources: Reporting metrics indexed as `truenas:pool` ancillary stats or `truenas:dataset` with ARC fields if exported
app: TrueNAS reporting API or `sysctl` scraper to Splunk
spl:
index=storage sourcetype="truenas:pool" earliest=-24h
| eval arc_hit=coalesce(arc_hit_ratio, arc_hits_percent, arc_hit_percent)
| eval arc_size=coalesce(arc_size_bytes, arc_size)
| timechart span=15m latest(arc_hit) as arc_hit_pct latest(arc_size) as arc_bytes by hostname
description: A falling ARC hit ratio on memory-bound appliances raises read latency for NFS and SMB clients even when disks are healthy.
value: Guides RAM upgrades and helps distinguish network issues from cache starvation.
implementation: If ARC is not in default pool payload, add a lightweight script querying `arcstats` via middleware. Document units in `props.conf`.
